programme.title: Serengeti
programme.desc: The aftermath of the devastating fire has left a blackened smoking wasteland, but rain soon brings hope to the scorched earth. Kike the cheetah and wild dog leader Jasari are both teaching their cubs how to survive on their own, only to be interrupted by a grieving giraffe. A baby elephant struggles in the flooded river, while Bakari returns when his family are threatened by a rival baboon troop. Lioness Kali has surprises in store which bring hope for the future of her pride, and as things draw to a close, a death brings all the families together in a remarkable truce(n)

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and Philip Mould investigate what could turn out to be one of the best bargains ever seen on the programme. Almost 30 years ago, Bob Kay bought a junk shop still life for £1. If it turns out to be by Giorgio de Chirico, it could be worth up to £50,000. Fiona and Philip visit Rome, where de Chirico lived for most of his life, and while they learn a great deal about the painter's status as a master of 20th-century art, they also discover he is widely faked. However, they do come across some interesting documents with which to begin their investigation(n)

programme.title: An Island Parish

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers forage for food, exploring Britain's fields and hedgerows in search of free ingredients that will form the basis of spectacular dishes(n)

programme.title: More Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Vet Megan treats a Great Dane with a life-threatening condition in Fife, and in the countryside Richard inspects a herd of cows to see how many are pregnant and fits nose rings to some of the bulls. Plus, Steve meets a rabbit with wonky teeth. Narrated by Felicity Kendal(n)

programme.title: Life Story
programme.desc: The importance of homes to animals, not only as a place to live but as a shelter from the elements and a refuge from enemies. A pack of African hunting dogs have made their home on a vast plain in Zambia, but it's far from safe as they must battle hyenas and protect their young. Hermit crabs on a tropical island settle down in empty snail shells - and when a new property washes ashore, they all form a queue, then move up the housing chain until all the shells are taken. On the edge of the Sahara are chimpanzees, whose survival relies on knowing where to find water in the most extreme droughts. Here the elders lead a troop on a brutal trek to a dried-out riverbed to dig wells(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Roo Irvine and James Braxton scour the shops of Liverpool and Manchester, where James makes a beeline for some tea making equipment, and Roo finds an enormous piece of ceramic(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: A team comprising children's TV presenter Barney Harwood, former world champion boxer Steve Collins, actor and impressionist Lewis MacLeod, Sky News weather presenter Nazaneen Ghaffar and One Show reporter Alex Riley takes on the general knowledge champions. Presented by Jeremy Vine(n)

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: British entrepreneurs' products are put to the test, with consumers unboxing, trying out and giving their feedback on each invention(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The team sets to work restoring four treasured family heirlooms. Metal worker Dom Chinea takes on the challenge of restoring an old, dilapidated barber's pole, while luthier David Kennett turns his attention to a broken Portuguese guitar. Elsewhere, Lucia Scalisi takes on the restoration of a Nigerian painting, and Will Kirk transforms a wooden writing slope that hid a life-changing secret(n)

programme.title: Remarkable Places to Eat
programme.desc: Nisha Katona takes Fred Sirieix to San Sebastian for a mind-blowing meal at one of the world's top restaurants and to visit a tiny sports bar to learn the secrets of the perfect tortilla. The first place in Nisha's little black book is Mugaritz, ranked as the ninth best restaurant in the world and famous for wild experimentation - something that is made clear as soon as the menus arrive. Instead of a list of dishes to select, the Mugaritz menu is two pictures, one of a swimming fish, one of a flying fish, and Nisha and Fred make their choice, but have little clue what they are going to eat(n)

programme.title: Animal Babies: First Year on Earth
programme.desc: Cameras follow six baby animals as they face the challenges of surviving their first year on Earth. In the final episode of the series, the six baby animals approach the end of their first year on Earth. In Iceland, an Arctic fox cub rejected by his mother must learn where to find food for the first time, while in Kenya, spotted hyena twin sisters must roam a territory they share with the biggest threat to their lives, lions. Whether breaking ties or building bonds, all the young animals must learn for themselves how to stay healthy and safe(n)

programme.title: Better Things
programme.desc: Sam asks Rich's advice when she has an anxiety attack about her new relationship, and Frankie invites her father to dinner(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef visits Vienna, the city that once ran the Austro-Hungarian Empire and continues to be home to comfort dishes such as tafelspitz and goulash(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emma Barnett(n)

programme.title: The Chefs' Brigade
programme.desc: Jason Atherton takes his group of chefs to Bergen, Norway, where they get an in-depth course in the specifics of Scandinavian cooking and ingredients as they prepare venison stew. A more experienced chef joins the brigade to help them rise to the challenge of preparing food for an award-winning restaurant, leaving some of the existing members fearful that they will be replaced(n)

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: Julian Peedle-Calloo and Yvonne Cobb travel to Reims in France to visit the famous Clin D'Oeil biannual arts festival, attended by thousands of deaf people from around the world(n)

programme.title: Remarkable Places to Eat
programme.desc: Leading chefs take maitre d' and First Dates star Fred Sirieix to experience dining at their favourite restaurants and explore what goes into making them so special. This time, chef Tom Kerridge takes Fred Sirieix to his favourite restaurants in Edinburgh, including fine dining restaurant Kitchin, ran by chef Tom Kitchin, where the pair enjoy a venison dish from the a la carte menu. The following day, Tom takes Fred to a restaurant that takes the connection between plate and local produce to a new level, before arriving at their final stop - a newcomer to the Edinburgh restaurant scene - housed in the recently renovated old Newhaven fish market(n)

programme.title: Imagine
programme.sub-title: Imagine - Faith Ringgold: Tell It Like It Is
programme.desc: Alan Yentob spends time with the artist, author and activist, following her in her New Jersey studio and in the heart of Harlem as she prepares for her show at the Serpentine Gallery in London. Ringgold painted some of the most truthful and empowering representations of African Americans during the civil rights and women's movements of the 1960s and 70s. Despite being dismissed by the art establishment, she told her story using numerous art forms, including traditional quilts which have their roots in the slave culture of the South. Ringgold re-interpreted their function to tell politically charged stories, something she has also done with her illustrated children's books(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Animal Park Summer Special

programme.title: An Island Parish
programme.desc: The Rev Richard Hines completes his final duties as he nears the end of his seven-year posting as rector of the largest Anglican parish in the southern hemisphere, while on Pebble Island Arina and partner Raymond prepare to vacate the farm that their families have had connections with for more than 100 years, destined for a new life in Stanley. Elsewhere, vet Steve Pointing tends to a penguin found covered in oil and taxidermist Steve Massam puts the finishing touches to his long-term project at the museum - a diorama of all the exotic wildlife to be found in the Falklands(n)

programme.title: This Farming Life

In Shetland, Kirsty and Aimee attempt a daring rescue mission when they realise a lamb has fallen off a steep cliff(n)

programme.title: Life Story
programme.desc: The importance of power in the animal kingdom, revealing how dominant creatures have the best access to food and water, and are also the most attractive to the opposite sex. An orphaned young chimp tries to climb the social ladder, but his troop is ruled by big, aggressive males and his first attempt to join them ends in a beating. A meerkat society will only allow newcomers in if they know how to deal with a venomous snake. And the only way to occupy the top spot in a meadow of kangaroos is to beat the ruling male in a boxing match - but few have challenged the current incumbent, who stands at 8ft. Will any animal dare take him on? David Attenborough narrates(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Antiques experts Roo Irvine and James Braxton visit the Midlands, where Roo takes a shine to some carriage lamps, while James meets his namesake and haggles over a box of fossils(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Stars of the small screen Jeremy Edwards, Chizzy Akudolu, Susie Amy, Mark Little and Hayley Tamaddon take on the general-knowledge champions to win a cash prize for charity. Jeremy Vine asks the questions(n)

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: British entrepreneurs' products are put to the test, with consumers unboxing, trying out and giving their feedback on each invention(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Stephen Kember is given a long-mute music box that once belonged to a professional stage magician and toy experts Julia Tatchell and Amanda Middleditch get their hands on a much-loved push-along horse that has seen better days. Other items up for repair are a water-damaged penny arcade game and an old leather-backed chair(n)

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Lynn and Sandra in the Highlands get a surprise when they harvest their first batch of honey. The Rodgers family gather to give bull Mario a good send-off, while Raymond and Johnny discover their sheep are under threat from flystrike, and the only way to protect them is to put the entire flock of 1,600 through the sheepdip(n)

programme.title: Broke
programme.desc: Further real-life stories of people who are in work but struggling to make ends meet. Intensive-care nurse Lorraine has not had a pay rise in eight years and with inflation and the rising cost of living, she earns thousands of pounds less after tax than in 2010. Kevin from North Ayrshire works more than 100 hours a week managing four businesses, yet he still delivers takeaways at night to save for his son's long-term future, while Eryl in Liverpool tops up her zero-hour wage by foraging for foliage and making wreaths to sell(n)

programme.title: Vic & Bob's Big Night Out
programme.desc: Vic Reeves and Bob Mortimer return in this one-off version of the 1990 show that originally introduced them to TV audiences - Vic Reeves Big Night Out. The duo promise new characters as well as a few old favourites, surprise cameos and a healthy dose of their trademark slapstick humour. Plus, a very popular television programme receives the R&M makeover treatment. Matt Lucas features among the jamboree, while Mel Gibson and Idris Elba are seen as never before(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Kirsty Wark(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available(n)

programme.title: The Conjoined Twins: An Impossible Decision
programme.desc: Conjoined twins Marieme and Ndeye have separate hearts and lungs, but Marieme's heart is very weak and her life expectancy is poor and if she dies, her stronger sister will die with her. As the girls' health deteriorates, their father Ibrahima and staff at Great Ormond Street Hospital are forced to decide whether a separation should be considered. This programme tracks the journey of Ibrahima and the medical team as they wrestle with is a seemingly impossible decision with the help of the hospital's Clinical Ethics Committee(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Proton beam therapy is the one of the most technologically advanced though expensive cancer treatments in the world - but it has the potential to save the lives of children with otherwise incurable forms of the disease. This programme follows the engineers, scientists and medics as they race to build two new centres, one at the Christie Hospital in Manchester and one at University College Hospital in London, as well as the first children awaiting the lifesaving treatment(n)

programme.title: Our Cops in the North
programme.desc: Observational documentary following the work of Northumbria Police across Newcastle, Sunderland and beyond, telling stories of the force's officers and victims of crime. In the second edition, police officers in Sunderland respond to the fatal stabbing of 62-year-old grandmother Joan Hoggett, and receive a call about a teenager covered in blood. Meanwhile, detectives work tirelessly to investigate a complicated crime spree, where offenders have stolen a pub jukebox, tip jar and charity box and while attempting to flee police have stolen a car and mugged a passer-by(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Animal Park Summer Special
programme.desc: Tragedy strikes in Koala Creek as kidney disease claims the life of Wilpena, but there is also happier news on the marsupial front when Ben and Kate catch up with wallaby Newt(n)

programme.title: Animal Park Summer Special
programme.desc: The vet is called when Violet the koala goes downhill, while Iggy Pop the iguana has a mystery tail injury that requires surgery - but at his age an operation is a risky business(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don and the team focus their attention on the loss of wildflower habitats in the UK and the part that gardeners can play in averting the resulting loss of wildlife. In Lincolnshire, Adam Frost finds out how crucial meadows are to supporting the ecosystem, Arit Anderson visits East Sussex to discover the results of a recent wildlife survey carried out in the gardens there and Nick Bailey travels to Staffordshire where meadows are being grown in gardens for the benefit of wildlife. Plus, Carol Klein identifies plants that can help increase insect and bird populations(n)

programme.title: BBC News at 9
programme.desc: No Details Available(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Curious Creatures
programme.desc: Lindsey Chapman and Martin Hughes-Games join team captains Chris Packham and Lucy Cooke on the panel show that tests wildlife knowledge, with Kate Humble asking the questions(n)

programme.title: Getting the Builders In
programme.desc: In Heaton Mersey, Manchester, Zoe wants her porch revamped and a new front door. She has £1,200 to spend and asks three pairs of tradesmen to put in a pitch(n)

programme.title: Len and Ainsley's Big Food Adventure
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott and Len Goodman visit east London, where the former Strictly Come Dancing judge returns to his childhood sampling some Jewish delicacies and has his first taste of Vietnamese food. Chef Ainsley heads to the kitchen and cooks a mouth-watering dish inspired by the day's adventures(n)

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers celebrate Britain's love of seafood and the plentiful and diverse supply of produce obtained from local waters. They also explore the history of Billingsgate Market in east London, the country's largest inland fish market(n)

programme.title: More Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Megan helps to rehome 80 caged hens with local families in Fife, and when one is discovered with a broken leg, she decides to take care of it. In Teesdale, farm vet Richard takes drastic action to save the life of a calf with bloat, while Helen meets a Jack Russell puppy that has bitten its own tongue. Narrated by Felicity Kendal(n)

programme.title: Life Story
programme.desc: David Attenborough reveals how the competition to breed creates scenes of the most extraordinary beauty, as well as some of the most violent battles seen in nature. A male flame bowerbird shows off its artistic prowess to impress a potential mate - but if that does not prove enough, it will demonstrate its dancing skills instead. Waved albatrosses pair up for life and spend hours in each other's company, although things are not so simple for a male peacock jumping spider - one wrong move in its dazzling courtship routine could prove fatal. However, one of the most magnificent displays of all is created by a tiny male pufferfish, which impresses the ladies by building a spectacular underwater 'crop circle' in the sand - arguably the most perfect and complex structure created by any animal(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: James Braxton and Roo Irvine head back up the north-west coast on the hunt for precious artefacts. En route Roo heads far out into Morecambe Bay to look for a local delicacy and James runs away to Blackpool to join the circus. Roo finds an object with equine interest, but will it make enough cash at the York auction for her to pip James to the greater profit(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Sophie Faldo, David Domoney, Frances Tophill, James Tanner and Will Kirk form another celebrity team aiming to triumph over the Eggheads. Presented by Jeremy Vine(n)

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: British entrepreneurs' products are put to the test, with consumers unboxing, trying out and giving their feedback on each invention(n)

programme.title: Monkman & Seagull's Genius Guide to Britain
programme.desc: University Challenge icons Eric Monkman and Bobby Seagull take a road trip with a difference, searching Scotland, England, Northern Ireland and Wales for hidden gems of British scientific and technological ingenuity. They begin with a trip to Blackpool Pleasure Beach to experience Sir Hiram Maxim's 1904 ‘Captive Flying Machines', followed by a visit to Southport, home to the only lawnmower museum of its kind in the world(n)

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys presents the first heat of the latest series of the classic quiz, where contenders sit upon the famous black chair under the glare of the spotlight and the ticking clock in an attempt to ultimately become the show's latest champion. The specialist subjects in this opening edition are the Lake District, the novels of Sue Townsend, horror writer Stephen King and celebrated chemist Dorothy Hodgkin(n)

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer brave the wild lakes of Essex in search of the mighty carp. They have the latest high-tech equipment to catch the biggest fish they can - and are fully prepared to stay all night if they have to. The pair discuss the true meaning of a mid-life crisis and decide to visit a beauty clinic to see whether there are any suitable treatments available to re-capture their youth. Bob reveals his secret recipe to make the perfect scrambled eggs - but it's not the only thing that shocks Paul(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty advises on essential summer pruning and adds French beans to the vegetable garden. The programme also meets a gardener in Hertfordshire who combines her love of cottage garden plants with tropical plants and visits a garden in Kent where pots are the stars of the summer show(n)

programme.title: Fosse/Verdon
programme.desc: The early 1970s finds Bob focused on editing the musical movie Cabaret. Gwen, meanwhile, auditions for the leading role in a straight play that forces her to reflect on the beginning of her career, her disastrous first marriage to James Henaghan and a career change that led to her breakthrough performance in Can-Can. Sam Rockwell and Michelle Williams star in this biographical drama detailing the lives of choreographer-director Bob Fosse and celebrated Broadway dancer Gwen Verdon. With Norbert Leo Butz and Peter Scolari(n)

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Joe Lycett, Phil Wang and Ellie Taylor join regular panellist Alan Davies, as questionmaster Sandi Toksvig looks at the pastimes of past times. As ever, correctness and even intelligence go out of the window as the panellists attempt to answer the fiendishly difficult questions, with points invariably awarded for the answers the questionmaster finds most interesting, as opposed to correct(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Sarah Smith(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available(n)

programme.title: Edinburgh Nights with Nish Kumar
programme.desc: Nish goes behind the scenes with some of the big names and hopeful first timers in comedy at the largest arts festival in the world. He samples the Los Angeles Philharmonic Orchestra playing for free in a football stadium, marvels at trail biking legend Danny MacAskill creating an extraordinary circus show and tries his hand at beatboxing(n)

programme.title: A Most Violent Year
programme.desc: New York City during the winter of 1981 is a dangerous place to be, but one immigrant is determined to expand his business and support his family. Unfortunately, the unrelenting rise in violent crime threatens to ruin his livelihood and destroy everything he has worked tirelessly for. Thriller, starring Oscar Isaac, Jessica Chastain and David Oyelowo(n)

credits.actor: Oscar Isaac, Jessica Chastain, David Oyelowo

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Venice 24/7
programme.desc: The emergency services monitor a religious festival that is being celebrated with a rave, leaving inebriated party-goers at risk of falling into the city's famous canals. Plus, paramedics struggle to reach an unconscious reveller, argumentative drinkers cause a problem, and a firework display brings the celebrations to a close with a bang(n)

programme.title: A History of Scotland
programme.desc: Archaeologist Neil Oliver explores more than 2,000 years of Scottish history, casting a fresh perspective on the events and characters of the country's past against a backdrop of stunning scenery and iconic landmarks. He begins by examining identity, starting in the first century AD, when the tribes of a place the Romans called Caledonia banded together to confront the legions of the Empire(n)

programme.title: The First Georgians: The German Kings Who Made Britain
programme.desc: Dr Lucy Worsley examines how George II had to adapt to a growing middling rank in society - who were no longer content with being subjects. Affairs of the state began to be openly discussed in coffee houses, and the king and his ministers were mocked in satire - with George becoming an easy target due to his frequent absences. His wife Caroline went on to popularise a medical breakthrough against smallpox, but it was their son Frederick Prince of Wales who had the popularity the monarchy needed to survive in the modern era(n)

programme.title: Mary Beard's Ultimate Rome: Empire Without Limit
programme.desc: Scholar Mary Beard takes an in-depth look at identity and citizenship within the Roman Empire, including what it meant to be, or become, Roman, and how the different parts of the Empire reacted to Roman rule. She follows the story of an African Roman from his native land to Britain, where he served as a governor, and in York and Newcastle she finds the remains of Romans, but not as one might imagine(n)

programme.title: Akala's Odyssey
programme.desc: The rapper and poet explores the world of Homer and the Odyssey, to discover how an epic poem became the cornerstone of Western literature and how his own experiences have been impacted by a 3,000-year-old classic. Akala travels on a personal journey from the ruins of Troy to the high city of the Acropolis, looking at how language can create national identity and how the Odyssey endures today(n)

programme.title: Tomorrow's Worlds: The Unearthly History of Science Fiction
programme.desc: Dominic Sandbrook concludes his exploration of the genre by considering the allure of time travel, with the power to change the past or see the future being a theme that writers and film-makers have embraced for many years. From HG Wells' pioneering scientist in The Time Machine, to Doc and Marty McFly in Back to the Future, audiences have been presented with a host of colourful travellers and their machines. Featuring contributions by former Doctor Who stars David Tennant and Karen Gillan as well as current showrunner Steven Moffat, actor Christopher Lloyd and screenwriter Bob Gale (Back to the Future), Quantum Leap star Scott Bakula and The Time Traveler's Wife author Audrey Niffenegger(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.sub-title: Horizon: How to Build a Time Machine
programme.desc: Time travel is not forbidden by the laws of nature - but to build a time machine, experts need to understand more about those laws and how to subvert them. Cameras follow scientists, including Professors Ron Mallet and Tamara Davies, who are working on the cutting edge of discovery - men and women who may discover how to build wormholes, manipulate entangled photons or build fully functioning time crystals. In short, enable an engineer of the future to build a machine that allows people to travel back and forward in time(n)

programme.title: A History of Scotland
programme.desc: Archaeologist Neil Oliver explores more than 2,000 years of Scottish history, casting a fresh perspective on the events and characters of the country's past against a backdrop of stunning scenery and iconic landmarks. He begins by examining identity, starting in the first century AD, when the tribes of a place the Romans called Caledonia banded together to confront the legions of the Empire(n)

programme.title: The First Georgians: The German Kings Who Made Britain
programme.desc: Dr Lucy Worsley examines how George II had to adapt to a growing middling rank in society - who were no longer content with being subjects. Affairs of the state began to be openly discussed in coffee houses, and the king and his ministers were mocked in satire - with George becoming an easy target due to his frequent absences. His wife Caroline went on to popularise a medical breakthrough against smallpox, but it was their son Frederick Prince of Wales who had the popularity the monarchy needed to survive in the modern era(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Gary Davies and Nicky Campbell introduce the edition from April 24, featuring Pat and Mick, James Brown, Fairground Attraction, Brenda Russell, the Primitives, Luther Vandross, Joyce Sims, Bananarama, Will Downing, S'Express and Scott Fitzgerald(n)

programme.title: Skies Above Britain
programme.desc: Air traffic controllers guide a passenger jet with a major technical fault safely to the ground, before responding to an unidentified flying object detected on radar screens. Meanwhile, British Airways Captain Paul Bonhomme takes part in an air race at Ascot, and a YouTube blogger realises he must undertake special training for his new camera-drone. Plus, a woman tries to overcome her fear of flying, and a man sets out to prove that he can learn to fly, despite having restricted growth(n)

programme.title: Operation Wild
programme.desc: Broadcaster Clare Balding and vet Steve Leonard travel the world to watch vets perform innovative life-saving operations on a variety of wild animals. This first edition features a baby panda called Zhu Xia in south-west China in need of an MRI scan, and rescued gorilla Shufai in Cameroon, who was shot by poachers when he was a baby. Plus, a manta ray on the Japanese island of Okinawa is given an ultra-sound scan to determine if it's pregnant, and a four-ton elephant in Laos with a gunshot wound(n)

programme.title: From Ice to Fire: The Incredible Science of Temperature
programme.desc: Physicist Helen Czerski explores the science of temperature. She begins by venturing to the bottom of the scale, revealing how cold has shaped the world and why frozen does not necessarily mean what people might think(n)

programme.title: Joan of Arc: God's Warrior
programme.desc: During the Middle Ages, a 17-year-old girl came from nowhere and fronted an army in a world that believed that women could neither fight nor lead. In this film, Helen Castor reveals the reasons Joan of Arc was able to do what she did in a tale of military and religious events mixed with psychological courtroom drama(n)

programme.title: The Secret History of My Family
programme.desc: The documentary charting social history over the centuries tells the story of an Edwardian family locked in benefit dependency, and of a mother who was accused of a shocking crime against her own child. Susan Nelson and her family live on government welfare - did her descendants follow a similar path or did they manage to move up in life? As the film reveals, more than 100 years on, some of them still rely on benefits, while others have freed their families from the grinding poverty of their ancestor(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Gary Davies and Nicky Campbell introduce the edition from April 24, featuring Pat and Mick, James Brown, Fairground Attraction, Brenda Russell, the Primitives, Luther Vandross, Joyce Sims, Bananarama, Will Downing, S'Express and Scott Fitzgerald(n)

programme.title: Immortal Egypt with Joann Fletcher
programme.desc: Archaeologist and historian Joann Fletcher explores the story of Ancient Egypt and begins with a search of the building blocks that made he civilisation so enduring. She discovers how its people changed from primitive farmers to pyramid builders in just a few centuries, and finds early evidence for Egypt's gods and its obsession with death and the afterlife. She also reveals how the first writing was used to calculate taxes and examines Egypt's first pyramid - one of the world's earliest stone structures(n)

programme.title: Secret Knowledge
programme.sub-title: Secret Knowledge: In Search of Rory McEwen
programme.desc: Jools Holland tells the story of his father-in-law, Rory McEwen, the aristocrat, artist, folk singer and pioneering TV presenter widely credited with introducing blues music to Britain. The Hootenanny star brings his relative's story to life with McEwen's paintings, archive material, and contributions from Van Morrison, David Dimbleby and Jonathan Miller(n)

programme.title: From Ice to Fire: The Incredible Science of Temperature
programme.desc: Physicist Helen Czerski explores the science of temperature. She begins by venturing to the bottom of the scale, revealing how cold has shaped the world and why frozen does not necessarily mean what people might think(n)

programme.title: BBC Proms 2019
programme.desc: John Wilson's virtuoso orchestra celebrates the music of one of the most famous film studios, Warner Brothers. The London Contemporary Orchestra performs excerpts from cult soundtracks together with recent works from Hans Zimmer and Mica Levi. programme.desc: When Lily is upset to realise Matt is going out with cool girl Greta Masters, Jas plans a make-over slumber party to cheer her up. Unfortunately it clashes with a rare comet sighting, which Martha and Ollie are desperate to experience. They have even been allowed to borrow the very precious school telescope for the evening. Ollie is smuggled in to Lily's house by Martha to watch secretly from the attic, with Martha planning to split her time between both events. After much chaos and confusion, and with the presence of Ollie in the house rumbled, a compromise is finally reached, where the girls all get to do both the make-over and watch the rare comet. At the end of the make-over session Martha and Jas are horrified to see Lily has transformed herself to look exactly like Greta in a bid to impress Matt. To spare Lily's feelings they pretend to like her new look, but are relieved the next day in school to see Lily returned to her normal, natural self. Lily has realised it's better to be(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Mick feels the pressure to sort out the Vic's finances, and is later distracted by Tina in her hour of need. Is he taking on too much? The police arrive in Walford looking for their suspect in Phil's attack, Billy struggles to keep his jealousy of Adam in check and Sheree has a suggestion to solve Denise's problems at the salon(n)

programme.title: Serengeti
programme.desc: The aftermath of the devastating fire has left a blackened smoking wasteland, but rain soon brings hope to the scorched earth. Kike the cheetah and wild dog leader Jasari are both teaching their cubs how to survive on their own, only to be interrupted by a grieving giraffe. A baby elephant struggles in the flooded river, while Bakari returns when his family are threatened by a rival baboon troop. Lioness Kali has surprises in store which bring hope for the future of her pride, and as things draw to a close, a death brings all the families together in a remarkable truce(n)

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and Philip Mould investigate what could turn out to be one of the best bargains ever seen on the programme. Almost 30 years ago, Bob Kay bought a junk shop still life for £1. If it turns out to be by Giorgio de Chirico, it could be worth up to £50,000. Fiona and Philip visit Rome, where de Chirico lived for most of his life, and while they learn a great deal about the painter's status as a master of 20th-century art, they also discover he is widely faked. However, they do come across some interesting documents with which to begin their investigation(n)

programme.title: Saving Britain's Worst Zoo
programme.desc: The documentary about the Tweedys' zoo nightmare picks up as news breaks of the shooting of the escaped lynx and the accidental death of another one. With debts in excess of £300,000 and their story making international news, they now face online death threats and calls for their zoo to be closed, while wildlife experts condemn the family and the council. The family face financial ruin and court proceedings. Can things possibly get any worse?(n)

programme.title: Murdered by My Father
programme.desc: A widowed father attempts to ensure his daughter is set up with the right husband. However, as her planned engagement approaches, she falls for a charismatic charmer and becomes caught in a conflict between two generations with differing values. Fact-based drama, starring Adeel Akhtar and Kiran Sonia Sawar(n)

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: Derek Brockway arrives in the Vale of Glamorgan on a walk of contrasts. On his 11-mile journey from Porthcawl to Southerndown he takes part in a lifeboat challenge, walks a guide dog puppy, sledges down a spectacular sand dune, rides a horse on Ogmore beach, and fishes off the rocks. Derek is also joined by a group of female walkers who end the series toasting marshmallows on the beach in the shadow of one of Wales' most stunning rock formations(n)

programme.title: Eisteddfod 2019 with Jason Mohammad
programme.desc: Part two of two. The presenter reports from the Conwy Valley, bringing highlights and stories from the National Eisteddfod of Wales. In this edition, he catches up with Wales rugby coach Robin McBryde who is the ceremonial keeper of the sword in the Gorsedd of the Bards, as well as renowned harpist Catrin Finch who is being inducted into this ancient order. Jason also discovers more about the iconic Welsh wrestler Orig Williams 10 years after his death as the Eisteddfod remembers a local legend. Plus, a visit to Maes B, the fringe youth festival, where the up-and-coming bands play alongside established acts(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Mick goes running but it is clear he is in pain - and things only get worse later when he collapses while referring the kids' football match. Iqra is suspicious of Habiba's sudden illness and catches her sister with Adam. Rainie tries to persuade Bobby to look after her, and Chantelle helps Whitney with her clothing empire, but is unhappy to see her images on social media(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Former Republic of Ireland midfielder Kevin Kilbane, jockey Sam Twiston-Davies, Olympic hockey gold medallist Kate Richardson-Walsh and Women's Rugby World Cup-winner Maggie Alphonsi join regular team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: Hold the Sunset
programme.desc: After the disastrous Open Day at his house, Phil is nowhere to be found. As Edith and Sandra hurry to Phil's house, Edith receives a phone call from her 'difficult' sister Joan inviting herself to stay. Sandra pulls faces, but Edith feels obliged to agree. The duo enter Phil's house where they find a piano-playing Queenie, but no Phil. Roger visits the recovering Bob in hospital and tells him that he has thought of a new way to make money and he has let his mother's spare room, online, to two Americans. Comedy, starring Alison Steadman, Jason Watkins, John Cleese and Sue Johnston(n)

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: It seems that everyone in the Brown family has a date for Valentine's Day except Agnes, so she turns to the internet to find herself a man. Dermot tries to come up with a loving gesture to prove to Maria he can still be romantic - but he makes the mistake of taking advice from Buster - while Agnes and Winnie are fascinated by Betty's sex manual and Grandad ends up addicted to Viagra. Hit comedy, starring Brendan O'Carroll(n)

programme.title: Hell On Wheels
programme.desc: Roller derby is one of the fastest growing sports in Europe. This documentary reveals the lives and loves of Wales' first and best roller derby team Tiger Bay Brawlers, following them through the biggest moment of their sporting lives - the European Continental Championships. To the uninitiated, roller derby looks like hell on wheels - a violent spinning vortex of tattooed women on roller skates, crashing into each other at high speed. To its devotees, however, it is more than just a sport(n)

programme.desc: A report on the fake alcohol being served to British holidaymakers in Greece that left one woman blind. programme.title: Shop Well for Less?
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Steph McGovern try to help the Miller family in North Lincolnshire, with parents Wes and Mel wanting to save up for their wedding after 10 years together. With no savings and big expectations, they need to make considerable changes if they want the wedding of their dreams - can Alex and Steph show these big spenders that it is possible to create memories without breaking the bank, and help them put money aside for their special day? The Great British public gets their say too, as they put four similar-looking biker jackets to the test(n)

programme.title: Sacred Wonders
programme.desc: From the ancient temples of Angkor Wat in Cambodia to the Shaolin Temple in China, discover what people do for faith in some of the most stunning sacred places on Earth. In the opening episode, in Cambodia at Angkor Wat, a man who believes the temples are home to his ancestral spirits risks his life to save them from the jungle, while at the Shaolin Temple in China, a Buddhist warrior monk faces a test that could change the course of his life forever(n)

programme.desc: The dry season is now upon the animals - a tough time for some, but for others a food bonanza. Jasari and his wild dog family are under siege from the hyena clan, so he must train his pups to survive, while Bakari the baboon is living in exile from the troop, only to spy the leader bullying his adopted baby. Kali and her cubs are enjoying life back in the heart of the pride, only to be attacked by the black-maned lions. But worse is to come when a storm ignites a devastating fire, consuming the dry land with flames(n)

programme.title: Serengeti
programme.desc: The aftermath of the devastating fire has left a blackened smoking wasteland, but rain soon brings hope to the scorched earth. Kike the cheetah and wild dog leader Jasari are both teaching their cubs how to survive on their own, only to be interrupted by a grieving giraffe. A baby elephant struggles in the flooded river, while Bakari returns when his family are threatened by a rival baboon troop. Lioness Kali has surprises in store which bring hope for the future of her pride, and as things draw to a close, a death brings all the families together in a remarkable truce(n)

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and Philip Mould investigate what could turn out to be one of the best bargains ever seen on the programme. Almost 30 years ago, Bob Kay bought a junk shop still life for £1. If it turns out to be by Giorgio de Chirico, it could be worth up to £50,000. Fiona and Philip visit Rome, where de Chirico lived for most of his life, and while they learn a great deal about the painter's status as a master of 20th-century art, they also discover he is widely faked. However, they do come across some interesting documents with which to begin their investigation(n)

programme.desc: Eileen quizzes Jan about Seb, but he insists he was working in Birmingham, and Gary searches for his lost money in the flower shop. Meanwhile, Toyah frets about the flat purchase, unhappy at the thought of ripping off Claudia(n)

programme.title: The Best Little Prison in Britain?
programme.desc: The unlikely decades-long friendship between prison officer ‘Jonesy' and repeat offender burglar ‘Jonno', who are about to go their separate ways, in retirement and release to the outside world respectively. Meanwhile, a row has broken out over boiled eggs, setting Margo is on the warpath, and she is determined to root out the bullies who have taken more than their fair share(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Eileen is gutted when Jan tells her that he's a liar and a cheat and that he never cared for her. Meanwhile, Gary confronts Ryan over his apparent windfall, and Claudia announces that she is moving into her flat after all(n)

programme.title: The Widower
programme.desc: DS Henry raids Malcolm's home after hearing he's embezzling funds at an angling club, and finds stolen laptops as well as an unlicensed firearm - but legal technicalities result in the case being dropped. The detective then discovers Malcolm and Simone are planning a transatlantic trip and fears the killer could use this opportunity to murder his fiancee. Will he be able to warn her before it's too late? Fact-based drama, starring Reece Shearsmith, John Hannah and Archie Panjabi(n)

programme.title: Killer Women with Piers Morgan
programme.desc: Piers travels to Homestead Correctional Institution in Florida to meet Amber Wright, who, at the age of 15 conspired with a group of friends to murder her ex-boyfriend Seath Jackson, an innocent teenage boy who was beaten, shot, and knee-capped before his corpse was burnt and his remains thrown in a quarry. Later, Piers travels to meet Seath's parents in Summerfield, Florida, with their visceral anger standing in stark contrast to Amber's cool demeanour. It soon emerges that Amber was a central player in the plot to kill Seath, but will she take responsibility for her actions?(n)

programme.title: Police, Camera, Auction
programme.desc: An insight into the auctions where criminals' assets from their nefarious activities are sold at auction, with the proceeds usually going to help those whose lives have been blighted by crime. From sports cars and designer goods to outrageous bling and strange collectibles, cameras reveal the broad range of criminals' possessions seized by specialist police teams and put under the hammer. The auctioneers and police discuss their work, while those victims who will benefit from the money wait anxiously to see the outcome of the sales(n)

programme.title: Long Lost Family: What Happened Next
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell follow the stories of three families who have faced challenges building their new relationships. Brenda Rhensius's daughter Joanne has struggled with the new situation because of the loyalty she feels towards her adoptive mother, while Spencer Brown and his sister Margot have been gradually trying to bring their two worlds together. The third reunion was not originally shown and it is only three years that Hazel Stubbs Races and her son Kenneth feel ready to share their experience.(n)

programme.title: John Bishop's Ireland
programme.desc: The comedian travels to Portrush, one of Ireland's most northerly points, where battered by big swells and cold arctic winds, he goes swimming with a local swimming club. In Belfast, John discovers a unique taxi tradition in the Falls Road district, while in South Armagh he meets farmer Jas McDonald for an idiosyncratic take on the reality of living on the border. John performs his comedy routine along the way, from a small and cramped gig in Belfast through to the biggest stage in all of Ireland. He completes his journey in Dublin, capital of the Republic(n)

Poirot, who is guest of honour at the gathering, appears to have an open-and-shut case on his hands - but the sleuth finds himself up against one of his finest adversaries. Whodunit, guest-starring Sarah Miles (Ryan's Daughter), with David Suchet(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: There is excitement throughout the house and nearby village as the annual cricket match draws near, and the event brings out Robert's competitive side. Violet's great-niece Rose arrives, but a trip to London reveals there is more to her than meets the eye, while Matthew and Mary seem to be keeping secrets from each other. Thomas's future appears to be uncertain, but he may have found an unexpected ally in Bates(n)

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica learns that her friend Seth Hazlitt's rich cousin has been murdered and vows to get to the bottom of the mystery - and when she discovers the victim was breeding rare magnolias, she soon finds a motive for the crime. Angela Lansbury and William Windom star(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The rural bliss of Ferne Basset is rudely shattered by the discovery of an elderly resident's drowned body. But when Barnaby and Troy descend on the village to ferret out the culprit, they get caught up in simmering backstage rivalries at a local amateur dramatic production of Amadeus. John Nettles stars, with Daniel Casey, Sarah Badel, Janine Duvitski and Bernard Hepton(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A former vice cop is found beaten to death, setting Brooks and Devlin off on an investigation through London's seedy underbelly. The victim's widow and his former boss both seem to know more about the murder than they are letting on - but the trail eventually leads to a respectable children's clothes shop. Crime drama, with guest appearances by Sean Pertwee (Cold Feet), Lesley Manville (Cranford) and Juliet Aubrey (Primeval)(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: The discovery of a body in a shallow grave by the Thames prompts Brooks and Devlin to reopen an eight-year-old murder case, casting doubt on a conviction made by Steel at the time. When the imprisoned man, Luke Slade, is given leave to appeal, he represents himself in court - but what starts as a legal battle soon develops into a personal vendetta. Crime drama, guest starring Iain Glen(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A rehearsal of an amateur operatic production ends with Morse being arrested on suspicion of murder and suspended from duty pending further investigation. Stripped of official powers, he fights to clear his name - unaware he has been pencilled in as the real killer's next target. Guest starring Ian McDiarmid, with John Thaw and Kevin Whately(n)

credits.actor: Ian McDiarmid, John Thaw, Kevin Whately

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Carmel has a broken leg, but Gail fears she might have damaged the baby. Angie asks Neil to move in with her, and Reg is disappointed when Maureen seems evasive(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Mason thwarts a gang of safe-robbers and finds a bag of explosives in their abandoned van - but when the haul ends up in the wrong hands, it could spell disaster for Dawn's birthday party. Guest starring James Gaddas, with Joe McFadden, Derek Fowlds and Rupert Ward-Lewis(n)

programme.desc: The Annual Busmen's Ball arrives, and Jack arranges a stripper as part of the entertainment. Comedy, starring Reg Varney and Bob Grant(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Suzy gives Stan food from the canteen to take home - but it's stolen property. When Blakey discovers that Stan and Jack are biting the hand that feeds them, he vows to make trouble. Vintage comedy, starring Reg Varney and Stephen Lewis(n)

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: Henpecked George finally finds a job, but the line of work could make him unpopular with road users. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A philandering husband is found dying by the swimming pool after being shot during a house party at the home of Lady Lucy Angkatell. Whodunit, guest-starring Sarah Miles (Ryan's Daughter), with David Suchet(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The arrival of a traumatised and wounded stranger in Aidensfield coincides with the discovery of a woman's body on the moors, and sets off a treacherous chain of events that leaves one long-time resident's life hanging in the balance. Nicholas Farrell and Clare Holman guest star in the last-ever episode of the long-running police drama(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kim wants Frank to stop treating her like an invalid, Jack and Sarah have another go at skiing, and Rachel asks Alan for some bar work, but Lynn is not happy about it(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Joe is not pleased when Mark tell him he is going out with Lisa, Jack and Sarah's skiing plans are scuppered, and Carol attempts to use her 'healing hands' on Kim(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Gail is horrified when the police interview her over Carmel's accident, and when Andy starts back at Bettabuy he is impressed by checkout girl Amy Nelson(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Martin feels miserable and upset that no one believed him, Andy is pleased when Amy agrees to have lunch with him, and Maggie and Ken plan an Easter holiday in Brittany(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.sub-title: The Jewel Robbery at the Grand Metropolitan
programme.desc: On doctor's orders, the dapper Belgian sleuth visits Brighton for what he hopes will be a relaxing holiday, but it leads to one of the most absorbing cases he has ever faced - the disappearance of a cache of jewels. Mystery, starring David Suchet, with Hermione Norris, Philip Jackson and Sorcha Cusack(n)

credits.actor: David Suchet, Hermione Norris, Philip Jackson, Sorcha Cusack

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Robert and his family make their annual summer visit to his Scottish cousins at Duneagle Castle, leaving most of their servants at home. Edith discovers newspaper editor Michael Gregson is also on holiday in the area and may not be there just for the sketching and fishing, while personal servant O'Brien finds a kindred spirit below stairs in the form of her opposite number. Meanwhile, at Downton, Carson faces the task of keeping the staff focused on their work polishing the silverware and cleaning, but with the Granthams hundreds of miles away, minds begin to wander to other things, including the forthcoming Thirsk Country Fair(n)

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica Fletcher tries to clear a friend implicated in the theft of a priceless Egyptian relic - and a murder to boot. Whodunit, starring Angela Lansbury, James Read and Lee Meriwether(n)

credits.actor: Angela Lansbury, James Read, Lee Meriwether

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: A case from 13 years earlier returns to haunt Lewis when the evidence comes under review. Graham Lawrie was incarcerated in a secure psychiatric hospital after being found guilty of the murders of three police officers, but now the findings of the forensics lab have been called into question and an appeal has been launched. As Lawrie remains locked up ahead of his day in court, PC Mark Travis is lured to an isolated location with a hoax call and falls victim to a killer who uses the same weapon as that used in the original murders(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: As the Second World War continues to rage, Foyle investigates a pro-Hitler organisation he suspects may be linked to a mysterious death at the White Feather Hotel, while reluctant assistant Milner struggles to decide where his loyalties lie. Crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen and Anthony Howell, with Charles Dance and Maggie Steed(n)

credits.actor: Michael Kitchen, Anthony Howell, Charles Dance, Maggie Steed

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ivy wonders what has gone on between Martin and Carmel. Neil is certain he can make Denise love him. Audrey is flattered when Reg chats her up and offers her a job at Bettabuy(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Neil tells Denise that Angie does not mean anything to him. Martin informs Carmel she is wrecking his marriage. Audrey breaks the news to Alf that Reg has offered her a job(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A disgruntled client falls under suspicion when an insurance salesman is found murdered, but the victim's complicated private life gives Mason a lot to think about before the case is solved. Alf Ventress makes himself at home in the Aidensfield Arms while his house undergoes repairs, and soon overstays his welcome, while a drunk locked up in the cells for a night turns out to be a war hero friend of Sgt Miller fallen on hard times(n)

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica hits the road to promote a new book club, but promptly finds herself plunged into a messy case involving plagiarism, blackmail and murder. Angela Lansbury stars, with a guest appearance by Ian Ogilvy(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan's spirits are lifted by the arrival of a beautiful new waitress. Comedy, starring Reg Varney(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Jack and Stan volunteer Olive as supervisor for the depot nursery. However, when she breaks her glasses and has to go home for a spare pair the clueless duo are left holding the fort. Comedy, starring Reg Varney, Bob Grant and Anna Karen(n)

credits.actor: Reg Varney, Bob Grant, Anna Karen

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: Mildred faces the prospect of becoming a step-grandmother when George's past catches up with him. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.sub-title: The Mystery of the Blue Train
programme.desc: The sleuth finds himself in interesting company as English socialites and American millionaires rub shoulders on board a train bound for the French Riviera. Passengers include oil magnate Rufus Van Aldin (Elliott Gould) and his daughter Ruth (Jaime Murray) - to whom he has given a magnificent ruby known as the Heart of Fire. With members of the aristocracy crammed into a confined space with their husbands, lovers and a renowned gemstone, murder cannot be far away. With Lindsay Duncan, Roger Lloyd Pack and James D'Arcy(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Sergeant Nokes arrives to take charge after a lorry driver is savagely beaten and his consignment of condemned meat is stolen on the way to the incinerator, causing a health alert in Aidensfield. Peggy gets involved when she is asked to house-sit Mrs Grant's mansion and takes advantage of the opportunity to run a makshift B&B, only to find herself under arrest for handling stolen goods(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kim wants Frank to stop fussing over her, Joe encourages Mark to keep trying with Melanie, Carol annoys Michael and Lynn with her gossiping, and Alice is snatched by a stranger(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Nick is frantic as the strange woman continues to claim that Alice is not his daughter. Lorraine flirts with Mark, and Kim tries to talk to Frank about the miscarriage(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Curly takes Maureen Naylor on at Bettabuy, Percy is not happy when he is photographed having a manicure, and Angie throws a flat-warming party(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Reg wants to have Neil arrested, Denise swears to Angie that she has not been leading Neil on, Nicky worries that Martin and Gail might be getting divorced(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A young woman asks Poirot to investigate her father's murder - a 14-year-old crime for which her mother was found guilty and executed. The detective agrees to investigate, but warns his client that he may discover things she does not want to hear. David Suchet stars, with Rachael Stirling, Aidan Gillen, Aimee Mullens, Toby Stephens, Gemma Jones, Patrick Malahide and Annette Badland(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: It's a happy day for Downton as Anna's patience is rewarded, while Edith receives admiration from an unexpected quarter. Matthew refuses to back down over the estate management, but will his tactics pay off? Branson's plan for the christening causes consternation, as does the arrival of his brother, and O'Brien's motives are unclear as she counsels Thomas. Julian Fellowes' period drama, starring Joanne Froggatt, Laura Carmichael, Dan Stevens and Allen Leech(n)

credits.actor: Joanne Froggatt, Laura Carmichael, Dan Stevens, Allen Leech

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica joins forces with a Russian detective to solve a case of art theft and murder at a book fair in Miami. Whodunit, guest starring David Ogden Stiers and Andrew Robinson(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: When a spate of petty thefts culminates in a series of murders at a student hostel, Miss Lemon persuades the Belgian sleuth to investigate. Mystery, starring David Suchet, with Philip Jackson, Pauline Moran, Damian Lewis and Paris Jefferson(n)

credits.actor: David Suchet, Philip Jackson, Pauline Moran, Damian Lewis, Paris Jefferson

programme.title: Unforgotten
programme.desc: Crime drama, starring Nicola Walker and Sanjeev Bhaskar as detectives investigating a cold-case killing from almost 40 years earlier. The discovery of a skeleton in the cellar of a demolished building launches a murder hunt, headed up by DCI Cassie Stuart and DS Sunny Khan. However, all they have to go on is a car key found near the body, which leads them on a search to find out who he is, as well as how, when and why he died. WithTom Courtenay, Trevor Eve, Bernard Hill, Ruth Sheen, Gemma Jones and Hannah Gordon(n)

programme.title: Unforgotten
programme.desc: Having identified the body in the cellar as Jimmy Sullivan, Cassie visits the victim's mother, who is relieved that she might finally find out what happened to her son 39 years after he went missing. The detectives focus their investigation on the names in Jimmy's diary, who are revealed to be a government advisor, a priest, a retired bookkeeper and a housewife with a secret past. What part, if any, could these four wildly different people have played in Jimmy's disappearance and demise? Nicola Walker and Sanjeev Bhaskar star in the crime drama, with Tom Courtenay, Trevor Eve, Bernard Hill and Ruth Sheen(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: The evacuation of a central London hospital leads to the discovery of a tragic death. When Brooks and Devlin investigate, a suspect is arrested but the prosecutors (Ben Daniels and Freema Agyeman) face an uphill struggle building a case - so they team up with the detectives in a bid to gather more evidence. Patrick Malahide and Bill Paterson co-star(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks and Devlin fail to find a lead when a 13-year-old is kicked to death at Euston Station, so they turn to Chandler, who holds a press conference. As the detectives are led through a tangled web of family loyalties, the suspect's barrister, an old flame of Steel's, gathers evidence for an audacious defence strategy that could change the nature of the British legal system. Dervla Kirwan guest stars, with Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Ben Daniels(n)

credits.actor: Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber, Ben Daniels

programme.desc: Counterfeiters lead Simon a merry dance from London to Switzerland via Paris, and the involvement of a bogus countess only complicates matters further. Roger Moore stars, with Kate O'Mara and Philip Madoc(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: Several men commit suicide at the same hotel - and Theo suspects they were murdered as part of a plot to avenge a woman who died under similarly suspicious circumstances(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: The coroner sets out to improve his bedside manner by spending time with a doctor, learning a valuable lesson in humility from a mother dying of a terminal illness(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: A criminal escapes from Chelmsford prison protesting his innocence and hoping to get the media's help to secure a release date - so Arthur tries to make money by selling his exclusive story to the highest bidder in Fleet Street. George Cole and John Labanowski star(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A series of robberies is committed on affluent couples travelling down a country lane, and it transpires two of the suspected offenders are wealthy themselves. Lesley-Anne Down guest stars alongside John Thaw and Dennis Waterman(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: A stakeout in a bowling alley escalates into a full-scale alert when an atom bomb is discovered, primed and ready to detonate. Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins star in an instalment featuring a fleeting early appearance by Pamela Stephenson(n)

programme.title: The Saint

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle are assigned to protect Cowley's former girlfriend, now a controversial evangelist, from an assassin, but are unaware that the would-be hitman is right under their nose. Drama, starring Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: With Haskins in Toronto, his stand-in DCI Quirk (guest star Bill Maynard) becomes obsessed with catching a gang of thieves. When information is received suggesting the crooks are about to rob a jeweller's shop, officers are poised for action, but events take an unexpected turn as the informant seemingly has a score to settle with Quirk first. Hard-hitting police drama, with John Thaw and Dennis Waterman(n)

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Extreme angler Jeremy Wade visits the Parana River in Argentina to investigate the death of a girl thought to have been killed by a creature the locals call the 'river dog'. Armed with a razor-sharp blade tipped with tissue-destroying venom, the species lives 500 miles from the sea and proves to be the largest totally freshwater river monster Jeremy has ever caught(n)

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Jeremy Wade heads to Thailand in search of the giant freshwater stingray, one of the world's largest and most terrifying river fish. Armed with a venomous 10in barb on the end of its whip-like tail, this 700lb predator tests the extreme angler to his physical limits, eventually snapping his rod and ripping his bicep(n)

programme.title: Fishing Allstars
programme.desc: Dean Macey and Adam Rooney travel to the Rio Ebro in Spain, where they target specimen roach and carp, and face the challenge of catching a giant wels catfish in a test of their skill and strength. They are joined on the final leg of their journey by globetrotting angler Gary Newman(n)

programme.title: The Living Daylights
programme.desc: James Bond crosses the continents to help a KGB agent to defect to the West. While protecting him from an unknown assassin, the super-spy is drawn into the world of arms dealing and a plot to trade millions of pounds' worth of diamonds for weapons to supply mercenaries around the world. Action adventure, with Timothy Dalton making his debut appearance as 007, alongside Maryam d'Abo, Joe Don Baker and Art Malik(n)

programme.title: FYI Daily
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest entertainment news(n)

programme.title: The Living Daylights

programme.title: Lethal Weapon
programme.desc: Now Murtaugh has met his match in the shape of former international CIA agent Wesley Cole, the newly melded partners attempt to find a steady rhythm as they work to solve the murder of a finance executive. Cole, who has returned home to assumes duty as an LAPD officer, also has to navigate his role with his ex and his daughter. Meanwhile, Avery grapples with the outcome of his campaign for city council. Damon Wayans, Seann William Scott and Kevin Rahm star in the fast-paced detective drama based on the hit feature films(n)

programme.title: Billy Connolly's Tracks Across America
programme.desc: In Portland, Oregon, the comedian meets Farmer Tom, a prize-winning vegetable farmer who has turned his green fingers to organic cannabis growing. His next stop is the stunning California coast - home to a cacophonous elephant seal rookery - the largest in the world. From there, he heads to the deserts of Arizona to meet the men and women on the frontline of US border control. Deep underground, Billy learns of America's biggest cold war secret and meets the former wing commander who literally had her finger on the nuclear button(n)

programme.desc: Tim Vine hosts the comedy quiz, as teams of fans of Dulwich Hamlet and Manchester City, managed by Paul Sinha and Sam Quek respectively, answer football-related questions(n)

programme.desc: Action from day one of the Brisbane Darts Masters, which featured the first-round matches at the Brisbane Convention & Exhibition Centre in Australia. programme.desc: Chris sets out on a mission to cross three former Soviet republics entirely by rail in just one week, travelling through Estonia, Latvia and Lithuania and uncovering amazing stories and dark secrets from the region's past. programme.title: The Murder of Charlene Downes
programme.sub-title: Vanished: The Murder of Charlene Downes
programme.desc: On November 1, 2003, 14-year-old Charlene Downes left her home in Blackpool and was never seen again. This documentary examines the events that followed. A wall of lies and misinformation hindered the subsequent investigation into Charlene's disappearance, until one man stepped forward. The information this witness gave to the police unveiled what was considered to be Britain's first grooming-gang scandal. It also enabled Lancashire Police to launch its biggest-ever investigation, including secret surveillance of two new suspects(n)

programme.desc: The doctors and nurses of Horfield Health Centre turn their attention to patients dealing with persistent pain, both physical and psychological. Dr Tom Pelly helps Vera, who is worried that her bad shoulder might be a sign of worse things to come, and Dr Alison Bolam solves the mystery of why visually impaired patient David is experiencing pain in his eyes and face. Dr Jane Edge believe impetigo might be the cause of Agnese's woes, and Dr Elizabeth Barnard suspects Hannah's abdominal pains might be something far more serious than a case of gastroenteritis(n)

programme.title: Chris Tarrant: Extreme Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Chris explores five countries that were part of Yugoslavia, which had an extensive state-run railway network before its break-up in the 1990s and the Balkan Wars of Independence that followed. From Slovenia, Chris heads east along an old Austro-Hungarian railway that was once used by the Orient Express, pays a visit to a very unusual railway-related battlefield in Bosnia and rides on one of the world's great mountain railways on his way to the Serbian capital Belgrade before finishing his journey in Montenegro(n)

programme.title: Britain's Parking Hell
programme.desc: The documentary about the fight to find a parking space focuses on the increasing use of technology. The West Midlands traffic police are assisted by Robocop, showing motorists how to park safely, and mum-of-four Davida meets the robots as she puts the UK's largest fully automated car park to the test(n)

programme.title: Vegas 24/7
programme.desc: Documentary meeting some of the characters and hearing the stories that make the gambling capital of Las Vegas one of the most fun-filled cities on Earth. A high-rolling millionaire arrives from Texas to hit the roulette tables and gamble tens of thousands of dollars. But luck is not on his side as he loses spin after spin. Meanwhile, theatre impresario David Saxe searches for new talent for his long-running production Vegas! The Show, while over at a pool party, the staff are topless and spending thousands of dollars on girls and champagne is just a normal day(n)

programme.title: Portillo: The Trouble with the Tories
programme.desc: Part two of two. The former cabinet minister's assessment of the Conservative Party concentrates on Brexit, and how the party has brought itself to the brink of political destruction over arguably the biggest political crisis in Britain since the Second World War. With contributions from Boris Johnson, William Hague, Michael Gove, Michael Heseltine, Norman Lamont and Tony Blair, Portillo considers how splits in the party over widened since the referendum, and how Parliament struggled to deliver on the British people's vote to leave the EU(n)

programme.title: When... Goes Horribly Wrong
programme.sub-title: When Luxury Holidays Go Horribly Wrong
programme.desc: When holidays go right they offer much-needed rest and relaxation in the lap of luxury. However, those same vacations can just as easily end in disaster. Examples include chartered accountant Bill and his wife Tanya, who paid £11,000 on a once-in-a-lifetime cruise to the Bahamas, only to end up trapped in the eye of a cyclone. Iain and Judie's luxury dream trip to Vegas turned into deadly nightmare when they were caught up in the now infamous shootings in October 2017 in which 58 people were killed. Contributors include Danni Menzies, Scarlette Douglas and Simon Calder. Narrated by Glynis Barber(n)

programme.title: In Therapy

programme.title: Great Artists
programme.desc: Tim Marlow charts the life of Greek artist Domenicos Theotocopolous, dubbed El Greco, one of the most distinctive painters of the 16th century. His work was considered to be ahead of its time - so much so that it was all but ignored for 300 years. El Greco developed a highly individualistic style that eventually proved enormously influential, paving the way for the Baroque and providing the inspiration for 20th-century expressionism(n)

programme.title: Chris Tarrant: Extreme Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Chris travels through the Middle East to explore what remains of the colonial railways, which were built there more than 100 years ago. In the Wadi Rum valley, Chris boards a steam train along one of the few bits of the Hejaz railway that remains in working use, following the route through Jordan, before crossing the border into Israel as he heads for Jerusalem, which is sacred to more than half of the world's population. Along the way, Chris visits the ancient city of Petra, as well as Haifa and Tel Aviva(n)

programme.title: Crossrail: Where Did It All Go Wrong?
programme.desc: Crossrail is Europe's biggest engineering project, a new train line linking east and west London that was meant to open in December last year, but it is years behind schedule, billions over budget and dogged by all kinds of problems. This documentary tracks the troubled project from the very beginning, featuring interviews with former workers, politicians and transport experts, as well as people who were promised that Crossrail would bring huge benefits(n)

programme.title: Celebrity 5 Go Barging
programme.desc: In Warwickshire, Michael Buerk, Shaun Williamson, Amanda Barrie, Anita Harris and John Prescott settle into new boats, then head out to conquer the Grand Union Canal. First, however, there is the small question of the 21-lock flight at Hatton as the five meet some of the volunteers who restore the locks and keep then functioning. Amanda takes a detour to Warwick Castle in search of her ancestors, while Sean and John, discover the unique joys of sheep-racing. Deep into Shakespeare country, Michael tries to find the famous local pies he remembers from his youth(n)
