Elsewhere, Penny has second thoughts about quitting her job as a waitress when her car dies, while Amy tries to find Raj a date. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco(n)

credits.actor: Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons, Kaley Cuoco

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon receives a nasty surprise when he visits his mother in Houston, while Raj's murder mystery party causes conflict for the rest of the gang members. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco(n)

credits.actor: Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons, Kaley Cuoco

programme.title: Celebs Go Dating
programme.desc: Having been pied on his first day, it's an incredible turnaround for Lee Ryan from Blue as he becomes the first celeb this series to go out on a second date - as things go from strength to strength with make-up artist Sarah. At the agency, the dating experts dish out some tough love. Jack Fincham needs to talk less and listen more, while Megan Barton-Hanson is told she comes across as cold(n)

programme.title: Dumb and Dumber To
programme.desc: Dim-witted friends Harry and Lloyd are reunited after 20 years. When Harry discovers he has a daughter he never knew about, he breaks his friend out of a psychiatric hospital and they go on a road trip with the intention of fulfilling parental responsibilities neither of them is capable of. Comedy sequel, starring Jim Carrey and Jeff Daniels(n)

programme.title: Jamie's Comfort Food Bites
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver gives some of the nation's favourite dishes a twist, creating the ultimate toasted cheese sandwich(n)

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt investigates concerns about levels of naturally occurring arsenic in baby rice products, Jimmy Doherty examines Marks & Spencer's claim that they can trace all their beefburgers back to the cow, and Kate Quilton find out whether a parasite could be hiding in sushi. Programme exploring the secrets behind mass-produced food, meeting technicians, scientists, factory owners, growers and producers to find out exactly what goes into the groceries that end up on the supermarket shelves(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: The wedding season draws to close, but Dick and Angel need to look ahead to the winter. Dick ploughs up the wild flower meadow, a change that Angel does not entirely approve of, and the couple transforms the dilapidated coach house into a cafe, complete with a striking neon sign(n)

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: It's Danish Week, and the bakers face unfamiliar recipes as they battle to impress the judges. There's a bread challenge Signature, where they must demonstrate style as well substance. Paul Hollywood sets a spherical Technical - and with a place in the semi-final at stake, everyone is out to impress him and Prue Leith. For their final challenge, the bakers must deliver an elaborate pastry Showstopper fit for a Danish birthday party(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon O'Brien turns tank parts into garden furniture, while Henry Cole takes his life in his hands with a motorised skateboard(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest begins at North Bridge Street B&B in Hawick in the Scottish Borders, which is run by Eileen Walsh and her Portuguese partner Emanuel Forte. When the guests arrive, the purple-themed rooms cause a reaction, while one competitor's constant criticising irritates another(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest continues at the Golden Fleece in Ruleholme, Cumbria, run by chef Robert Cowan and his partner, Sylvia Spark. Before long, guests Eileen and Emmanuel pick up on cleanliness issues, and breakfast proves to be a disappointment(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to the Bubble Inn in Stenson, Derbyshire for the third day of the competition, where unsightly stains and security leave some of them less than impressed. Manager and ex-semi-professional boxer Connie Eleftheriou is keen to take his rivals in the ring with him, however the real damage happens when Emmanuel is taken to hospital after some overenthusiastic dancing at a Greek-themed night(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Melinda Beckett-Hughes is the final host of the week at Ayuda House in Greater Manchester - and as she has spent the week picking fault, the guests expect perfection from her. However, Robert and Sylvia are shocked to find a dirty towel in a drawer, while Emmanuel and Eileen cannot believe the levels of dust and Connie is horrified by the carpet(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they have been paid and settle some old scores. Eileen and Emmanuel from North Bridge Street want to get to the bottom of Melinda and Stewart's dirty bin comments as well as challenging Melinda's shower issues. Robert and Sylvia from the Golden Fleece try to defend their breakfast disaster, but also pull Eileen and Emmanuel up on their low score(n)

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Phil Spencer meets expectant parents Steve and Sally, who are relocating to the countryside around Stratford-upon-Avon in Warwickshire. They want to find their dream family home before the baby arrives, but with nearby rural walks, office space and a good village pub on the wish list, Phil has to act fast. Kirstie Allsopp is in Northamptonshire helping Rinku and Sunder. They have been living in a family owned property for the past three years, enabling them to save to buy a place together. However, there are new plans for the land and the bulldozers are inbound, so it's Kirstie's challenge to get the couple into their own home quickly(n)

programme.title: Love It or List It Australia
programme.desc: Natalie is over the dated kitchen and odd layout of the Coburg North home she has owned for 10 years and wants to swap it for an abode in the sunnier weather of Queensland, but Matt disagrees. Andrew Winter suggests a Brisbane alternative, while Neale Whitaker believes improvements could be made to the Melbourne property(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Chester Zoo's penguins move into a temporary home while a new luxury home is built for them, but the move becomes upsetting when malaria sweeps the colony, with elder Rudd battling to survive against the odds. Meanwhile, the African painted dog pups venture out of their den for the first time, but the runt of the litter Mosi refuses to go out into the cold, and keepers worry she may have issues with her eyesight. Elsewhere, Sumatran orangutan Emma gives birth, and a beetle with a wonky horn has to fight other bachelors in the group to win the attention of a female.(n)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel treats a Labrador suffering with severe arthritis in its elbow, and suggests using stem cells to help with the inflammation, while one of the team's senior neurologists deals with a Chihuahua born with fluid on the brain. Plus, a man comes all the way from France with his Labrador cross, which requires high-end surgery for elbow dysplasia(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick and Angel return to continue their renovation adventures in France. Two-and-a-half years have passed since the couple swapped their flat in Southend for a 19th-century chateau in France costing only £280,000, and in that time, the pair have worked to transform the derelict property into a fairytale castle. However, around half of the 45 rooms are yet to receive adequate attention, the couple's fledgling events business demands attention, and the outbuildings are still in need of conversion(n)

programme.title: Outlander
programme.desc: As Brianna grapples with the life-changing revelations of the past summer, Claire must help her come to terms with the fact that she is truly her 18th-century father's daughter(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Patients include 54-year-old John, who was involved in a cycling accident in which he went over his handlebars and was unconscious for 10 minutes. Sydney, who's 87, has reluctantly come to A&E after he experienced severe chest pains, while Alan has fallen and badly fractured his elbow(n)

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy panel show's version of the words-and-numbers quiz, with David Mitchell and Cariad Lloyd, taking on Jon Richardson and Russell Howard. Sam Simmons joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Beaver, Pennsylvania, where he meets a brother-and-sister team who find it almost impossible to work together. Their relationship is so fractured that they're failing to save their father's Italian restaurant Levanti's from being overwhelmed by local competition, and the situation has become so desperate that their dad is in danger of losing his life savings(n)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel treats a Labrador suffering with severe arthritis in its elbow, and suggests using stem cells to help with the inflammation, while one of the team's senior neurologists deals with a Chihuahua born with fluid on the brain. programme.title: Jamie's Comfort Food Bites

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty visits Sri Lanka to reveal the extraordinary lengths to which manufacturers go in order to split red lentils, and asks why they bother with this process. Kate Quilton learns why the way tea is drunk can affect its taste, and Matt Tebbutt finds out the difference between bubblegum and chewing gum(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: As autumn sets in, Dick and Angel harvest their pumpkins in the walled garden and organise a family wheelbarrow race. The basement is the latest room to be transformed, serving as a nerve centre for the whole chateau, a space for storing deliveries, and Dick's workshop. The old piggery is also renovated, in anticipation of some new arrivals(n)

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: It is semi-final week for the remaining contestants, who know they are just three patisserie challenges away from the biggest day in their baking lives. First comes the Signature and a deceptively simple challenge, with Paul Hollywood and Prue Leith looking for perfection, followed by Prue's complex, multi-layered Technical. The pressure intensifies when they are asked to create a spectacular Showstopper that could grace a Parisian patisserie - but who has the skill to reach the Bake Off final and who will fall at the final hurdle?(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon O'Brien converts a lantern into a stylish table and Henry Cole changes a fire extinguisher into a steampunk lamp as the pair visit a private railway(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest begins at Shannon Slater-Dent and Trevor Tredgett's Bove Town House in Glastonbury, but a poor night's sleep bothers Kathryn and Trevor from Treberthan B&B(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to Treberthan B&B in Par, Cornwall, where owner Kathryn Langley and her best friend Trevor Burnell set out to give their guests a taste of Cornish hospitality(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest continues at SugarBeat Eating House in Swainsthorpe, Norfolk, run by Stevie King and David Lovett, but the establishment's cleanliness quickly comes under fire(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit of the week is to the Fritton Arms in Norfolk, run by Ben Davenport and partner Sian Dewaal. David and Stevie are upset about their feedback so they conduct a forensic-style room inspection - complete with rubber gloves - and a paddleboat race on a nearby lake brings out everyone's competitive nature. Ben impresses the guests by revealing the hotel's aristocratic connections - but soon comes under fire for allowing pets to stay(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners reunite to find out what they have been paid, with Shannon and Trevor from Bove Town House wanting to discuss Kathryn's comments about her poor night's sleep(n)

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: The experts are in Manchester, where house prices have been known to rise faster than anywhere else in the UK, including London. Kirstie Allsopp is with Katherine and Andrew, who are looking for a property they can renovate into their dream home. They have £185,000 to spend - but money isn't Kirstie's biggest challenge this week - she's facing competition from developers and needs the couple to decide quickly when they see the place they want. Phil Spencer scours the southern suburbs for Sarah and Claude, who have their hearts set on desirable Didsbury. They desperately need more space as Claude's one-bedroom bachelor pad is bursting at the seams, but with their sights set on one of the most expensive areas in the city, Phil has to open their eyes to what their £250,000 budget can buy them in other locations(n)

programme.title: Love It or List It Australia
programme.desc: Andrew Winter and Neale Whitaker help a Brisbane couple who have just had a baby, with Nick wanting to find a spacious new home with a pool, while Bec would rather stay put(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Spider monkey Kiara becomes a mother for the first time, but keepers worry that the baby is becoming weak as Kiara appears to be more interested in playing with the other monkeys than taking on maternal responsibilities. Meanwhile, baby capybara Henry enjoys having the attention of all the female capybaras, and Babirusa Sausu moves from paddock to paddock to mate with various females(n)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A Great Dane comes to the clinic needing an operation on its spine, but when Noel Fitzpatrick discovers the dog has laryngeal paralysis, the prospect of surgery suddenly becomes much more complicated. There are also two Labradors in for treatment. One needs a knee replacement, while the other has a leg tumour that may require amputation. But there may be bad news(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick gets to work on the walled garden in the spring and buys a mini tractor for the job - but it may not fit through the gate. Angel designs another suite, with black walls and mosaic mirrors, and decorates the entire dining room with blossoms so a guest can make a special wedding proposal. The couple also consider the possibility of truffle farming(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Neil, who is 53, is rushed into hospital with severe chest and lung injuries after coming off his bike, and 40-year-old Michael is brought in with chest pain and a heart rate of up to 180bpm after collapsing on a golf course. Lastly, doctors treat 27-year-old George, who accidentally grazed his eye on a low hanging branch(n)

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: The devastating effects that so-called legal highs can have on users in one community and the huge strain their use is putting on already stretched emergency services. In Warrington, PC Karl Dickin is one of eight officers sent to detain a man in his 20s who's taken a cocktail of illegal and legal drugs and is acting erratically, while a regular user is found coughing up blood at a bus stop(n)

programme.title: Shocking Emergency Calls
programme.desc: A dramatic 911 call reveals that a helicopter crash has taken place as wealthy entrepreneur Charles Burnett III and his friends fly to his ranch in New Mexico(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay visits Sam's Mediterranean Kabob Room in Monrovia, California, and within minutes of his arrival, is caught in the crossfire of an argument between the owner, his wife and their children, all of whom are feeling the pressure of working seven days a week. Can the chef save the business and mend the warring family's frayed relationships?(n)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A Great Dane comes to the clinic needing an operation on its spine, but when Noel Fitzpatrick discovers the dog has laryngeal paralysis, the prospect of surgery suddenly becomes much more complicated. There are also two Labradors in for treatment. One needs a knee replacement, while the other has a leg tumour that may require amputation. But there may be bad news(n)

Kate Quilton learns why the way tea is drunk can affect its taste, and Matt Tebbutt finds out the difference between bubblegum and chewing gum(n)

programme.title: Jamie's Comfort Food
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver puts his stamp on three comfort food classics. First up is a retro treat - chicken Kiev, before the chef prepares spaghetti vongole with a non-traditional pink twist and a deluxe shepherd's pie made with slow-roasted lamb shoulder rather than mince(n)

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty is in the US state of Maryland to find out why soft-shell crab is such a delicacy, Kate Quilton asks what in nature provides the bright blue dye used in sweets and Matt Tebbutt reveals why there is jelly in pork pies and the lengths scientists go to in order to put it there(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge and Angel Adoree help British people making French castles their home or running businesses from them. In the opening episode, ther duo give Jonny a crash course in the dos and don'ts of chateau shopping before he and his wife start the search for their own dream place. Jonathan and Michael, who are struggling to turn their magnificent French castle into a money-making venture, explore some of their chateau's caves in the hope of finding a buried fortune. Plus, Phil and Angelina struggle to pitch their chateau as the perfect place for a 50th wedding anniversary party when they can't see eye to eye themselves(n)

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.sub-title: The Great British Bake Off: The Final
programme.desc: Noel Fielding and Sandi Toksvig present the 2018 grand final, as the three remaining bakers compete to impress judges Paul Hollywood and Prue Leith and take home the trophy. For the final three challenges, they must prepare and decorate doughnuts, followed by a deceptively simple technical, and climaxing with an intricate, multi-layered, edible landscape showstopper(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Max McMurdo restores a pair of designer chairs, while Henry Cole breathes new life into a decrepit garden table, transforming it into a colourful and valuable new one(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest begins at the Richard III in Middleham, North Yorkshire, a pub run by Ken Ward and his wife Sharon, who become suspicious when one couple check out before breakfast. The village is known for its horse racing links, but one of the competitors, Matt Wilson, has a fear of horses, and another couple are concerned at staying above a noisy pub(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to the Court House Unique Boutique B&B in Shelsley Walsh, Worcestershire, where hosts Keith and Lisa Shearin hope to impress. On arrival, Ken and Sharon are blown away by their honeymoon suite and the seemingly low price. Suspicions over price fixing drive Matt and Paul to the internet to verify the room rate, where they discover an anomaly, and despite Keith's best efforts at breakfast, Jo and Louis dismiss it as being too fancy(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to Castley Camp in Otley, West Yorkshire, for the third day of the contest, owned by single mum Jo Stevenson and her son Louis. Some of the guests are horrified by stains on their bedding and dust in their room, while an afternoon boot camp fitness session reveals Keith's competitive side. At feedback, Jo is left fuming over the comments about cleanliness and breakfast(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final two days see the contestants staying the night at the Lion & Swan in Congleton, Cheshire, run by owner Paul Whyle and manager Matt Wilson. A dirty bathroom and a waterbed cause upset, while some guests are still fuming about previous negative feedback. However, the worst is left for breakfast, when Sharon reveals she has discovered their rooms online at a higher price than they were told, upsetting Keith and Lisa and leaving the hosts determined to clear their names(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they've been paid and to settle some scores. First up are Ken and Sharon from the Richard III, who want to learn more about what's been causing Jo and Louis to lose sleep. Next, Keith and Lisa from the Court House Unique Boutique B&B want to challenge Matt about the allegations of price fixing and vehemently fight their corner. Needless to say, there is tension in the air and axes to grind, before the winner is revealed(n)

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: The property experts visit south London, as Phil Spencer helps childhood sweethearts Cleo and Ben find a home together in Brixton on a budget of £500,000. Meanwhile, Kirstie Allsopp meets first-time buyer Charli, who wants to get her foot on the property ladder in and around Croydon, with £280,000 to spend(n)

programme.title: Love It or List It Australia
programme.desc: Mel and Macca have nine children and Mel feels that all the space in their home at Park Orchards, a Melbourne suburb, is in the wrong place. Neale Whitaker has to re-design the house to make it open plan and add bedrooms, while Andrew Winter must pry Macca away from what he already thinks is his perfect home(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Documentary revealing the hidden lives of the animals of Chester Zoo, one of Britain's most popular zoos, using micro-rig technology to capture the behaviour of the animals and their close relationships with their keepers. In the first episode, the zoo's Asian elephant family welcome a new arrival. Grumpy matriarch Thi has been pregnant for almost two years and is reaching the end of her tether. She's given birth to a stillborn baby in the past, so the keepers have a close eye on her. Cameras also follow Red panda Jung, who has been getting the cold shoulder from his partner Nima since they had two cubs(n)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A two-year-old Bengal cat is rushed in after being hit by a car, and when it turns out to have a broken back and a dislocated spine, Noel Fitzpatrick agrees to do what he can - but he makes no promises. A German shepherd requires a total elbow replacement, a difficult operation using a custom printed implant system, and a rottweiler puppy is treated for a genetic condition that has destroyed the cartilage on its ankle bone(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick and Angel look ahead to wedding season, with eight couples booking the chateau for their big day. Dick also converts a stone barn into a flat for Angel's parents, getting rid of 150 years' worth of rubbish in the process, but tensions rise between the couple when he gets carried away with the trees at the front of the property(n)

programme.title: Thou Shalt Not Kill
programme.desc: Drama focusing on the affairs of determined police officer Valeria Ferro, who seems to possess a sixth sense when solving the puzzles behind crimes motivated by jealousy, vendettas and repressed anger among closed social groups. The body of a 15-year-old girl is found in a flooded basement just as her father and diving coach is at a television studio where he has gone to make an appeal regarding her disappearance. In the homicide division of Turin's police force, Inspector Ferro grabs the reins of the investigation. Miriam Leone stars. In Italian(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Zac, who's 30, is rushed to hospital after severely dislocating and fracturing his ankle playing football with friends. He arrives in shock and pain and doctors have to work swiftly to restore the blood flow to his foot and reduce his pain. Meanwhile, doctors treat Jayden, who has a swollen eye, and Ian, who fell off the tailgate while unloading his lorry(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home(n)

programme.title: Steve Backshall's Extreme Mountain Challenge
programme.desc: Part two of two. The naturalist reaches the peak of one of Venezuela's tepui mountains, before joining a party of Italian cavers as they venture into the unexplored caverns within. Inside, they discover strange wildlife and evidence of the geological forces that give the tepuis their unusual shape. Finally, the team returns to the ground by abseiling down the Angel Falls, the world's highest waterfall(n)

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: The guys go big in Texas with a mega pick at the first Dr Pepper bottling plant, and tough negotiations in a 50,000 square-foot warehouse(n)

programme.title: Storage Hunters UK
programme.desc: Linda needs help shifting some heavy gear in Carmarthen, but it remains to be seen whether Scotty will help out. Sean Kelly presents(n)

programme.title: Storage Hunters UK
programme.desc: One unit in Cardiff has the bidders all gagging. Sam is determined not to be outbid by the other women and Scotty and John try to fathom why Heavy D has come dressed as a Ninja(n)

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: In California, the guys deal with a collector who is under pressure to downsize, and later play a hunch that turns up a lot of rare vintage cars(n)

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: The guys meet a man who had pieces on display in the Smithsonian, but find that what he has held on to is even more mind-blowing(n)

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: The guys pick the collection of a reclusive and mysterious businessman named The Colonel, who has gone to great lengths to keep his identity a secret(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The presenters embark on a road trip across Australia's Northern Territory in fast, luxurious GT cars, with Jeremy Clarkson choosing a BMW M6 Gran Coupe, Richard Hammond picking a Bentley Continental GT V8 and James May opting for a Nissan GT-R. The trio set out on an adventure that takes in crocodile-infested rivers, a race around a vast opencast mine and an encounter with one of the world's longest lorries, concluding with an unusual challenge involving a farm, their cars and 4,000 cows. Plus, 24 star Kiefer Sutherland takes to the track in the Reasonably Priced Car(n)

programme.title: Cops UK: Bodycam Squad
programme.desc: Documentary series following the work of the Staffordshire Police force. Rookie cop Ash is in the thick of it during a late night brawl in Stoke-on-Trent(n)

programme.title: Yianni: Supercar Customiser
programme.desc: This time, Yianni Charalambous customises reality television star Sophie from Geordie Shore's Velar car(n)

programme.title: Yianni: Supercar Customiser
programme.desc: How do you improve upon perfection? Custom car king Yianni Charalambous is challenged to create a new look for a million-pound Pagani Huayra(n)

programme.title: Modern Wheels or Classic Steals
programme.desc: Two classic and two modern convertibles are pitted against each other, as Elo and Rebecca try to find something stylish and sexy to meet Katie's requirements(n)

programme.title: Modern Wheels or Classic Steals
programme.desc: Rebecca Jackson and Elo help a car buyer look for the perfect 4x4, and the vehicle must be suitable for the buyer's canine companions(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May attempt to improve the ambulance, with the presenters all going their own separate ways to enhance one area they think could do with an overhaul. James focuses on passenger comfort, Richard tries to shorten response times by getting other drivers out of the way more effectively, an issue Jeremy tackles with the invention of what he calls the 'sports ambulance'. Their three very different creations are then put to the test with a series of severe and often messy challenges. Plus, Formula One driver Daniel Ricciardo is the Star in the Reasonably Priced Car(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson tests the new BMW M4 at the track, before switching to the German manufacturer's hybrid-powered i8 for a trip up the A1 to Whitby, North Yorkshire, where he decides which of the two he would rather take for the drive home. With Land Rover production ceasing in the UK later this year, Richard Hammond pays tribute to the 4x4 by attempting to drive one straight up the side of a dam, while James May puts the Mercedes AMG GT through its paces. Taking to the test track in the Reasonably Priced Car are Will Smith and Margot Robbie(n)

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: The comedians are challenged to taste-test some mystery baby food, Russell Howard presents a nightmarish fairy tale and Liza Tarbuck introduces a plastic lizard to a cordless drill(n)

programme.title: Expedition with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve and a team of kayakers head to the Himalayas to visit the last great unexplored river in Bhutan. Legend has it that at some point, the river drops off the face of the planet(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.sub-title: Henning Wehn, Lucy Porter, Guz Khan
programme.desc: Henning Wehn hosts from London's Hammersmith Apollo, welcoming Lucy Porter and Guz Khan to the stage(n)

programme.title: Jon Richardson: Ultimate Worrier
programme.desc: Jon and guests Josh Widdicombe, John Robins and Holly Walsh discuss, categorise and rank People worries. Are greetings getting out of hand? Are robots doing all our thinking?(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.sub-title: Dara Ó Briain, Zoe Lyons, Paul Chowdhry

programme.title: WWI Aces Falling
programme.desc: The story of Edward Mannock and James McCudden, the British fighter pilots who rose from modest backgrounds to become two of the country's greatest First World War aces. But as their tally of victories grew, so did their chances of dying. Timewatch recounts Mannock and McCudden's battle to survive against the odds - and the 90-year mystery surrounding their deaths(n)

programme.title: Napoleon
programme.desc: Andrew Roberts' three-part profile of the French emperor concludes as the historian sheds light on Bonaparte's eventual downfall. He explores how a breach of trust by Tsar Alexander I led Napoleon to launch his ultimately disastrous 1812 invasion of Russia, and began a chain of events that would lead to the erosion of his support across the continent, culminating in his decisive defeat at the hands of the Duke of Wellington during the Battle of Waterloo. Andrew reveals how the monarchies of Europe banded together to bring down the French emperor, and then visits the remote Atlantic island of St Helena, where the British eventually imprisoned Napoleon following his defeat in the Netherlands(n)

programme.title: Time Team
programme.desc: Tony Robinson and the team embark on their 200th dig, examining the bed of a reservoir in a valley high up on Dartmoor in Devon. The specially drained site presents difficulties with its abundance of thick mud, but the archaeological experts uncover a network of monuments that suggests the existence of a major prehistoric site. Their efforts are further rewarded with the discovery of the first stone henge in Britain for a century(n)

programme.title: Time Team
programme.desc: Tony Robinson and the archaeologists excavate a site in Leicestershire, which they believe contains an Anglo-Saxon burial ground. Though their initial search for a cemetery proves fruitless, more digging leads to the experts uncovering evidence of several types of funeral, as well as a highly valuable piece of jewellery - and one member of the team volunteers to take part in a re-creation of a cremation ceremony from the period(n)

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: British bombers are unable to hit their targets precisely and attack German cities under cover of darkness, while the Blitz in Britain brings public demand for revenge strikes, and Arthur Harris takes charge of Bomber Command(n)

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston investigates one of history's most enduring mysteries as he goes on a world-wide search for the Holy Grail, visiting Glastonbury in Somerset, Wales, Valencia in Spain, and New York City to inspect four goblets that could be the ancient relic(n)

programme.title: Walter Tull - Forgotten Hero
programme.desc: Former EastEnders actor Nick Bailey investigates the story of Walter Tull, the pioneering black British footballer who died heroically fighting in World War One. He examines how Walter became the first black officer in the British Army when regulations required men of pure European descent, asks why he was denied the honour of a Military Cross for heroism and explores how he overcame racial abuse(n)

programme.title: Time Team
programme.desc: The archaeologists get an opportunity to dig at an army firing range in High Ham, Somerset, to investigate a set of Roman mosaics first discovered 150 years ago. Initial searches suggest the remains of Roman villas could be located on the site, and the team begins to build up a picture of the highs and lows of life in the society of the time - until the cold weather threatens to bring the excavation to a premature halt. Presented by Tony Robinson(n)

programme.title: Time Team
programme.desc: Tony Robinson gets to choose a site for investigation and decides to take the team to Jersey, where a German anti-aircraft battery was built during the Second World War. As the experts explore the area, they realise it contained a large and complex settlement, and discover shocking facts about the islanders' suffering during the war(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Documentary exploring how recent archaeological discoveries have shed new light on culture, origins and capabilities of the very first native Britons. For centuries, it has been assumed Britain's first peoples lives a brutal, hand-to-mouth existence as hunter-gatherers, but fresh evidence has emerged that has forced scientists and anthropologist to reassess their impressions of who these people were. The result is a very different portrait, illustrating a hardy and sophisticated culture that withstood centuries of extreme climate change, including a devastating tsunami, and narrator Adrian Lester describes how these early Britons endured the primal forces and became the first people to populate the island nation that would eventually become known as Britain. Includes contributions by a variety of experts, including Dr Hans Peeters, Dr Jim Innes, Professor Vince Gaffney, Dr David Jacques and Professor Martin Bell(n)

programme.title: Raiders of the Lost Past
programme.desc: Explorer Hiram Bingham's quest to find a settlement deep inside the Amazon jungle said to have been built by the great Inca Empire after its members fled from the ferocious onslaught of Spanish Conquistadors in the 16th century(n)

programme.title: Time Team
programme.desc: Tony Robinson and the team travel to Derwentcote in Co Durham, where they explore the Industrial Revolution's impact on the region by digging through the undergrowth to search for old furnaces and forges. Their investigation is also helped by the diary of an 18th-century industrial spy, and records from the time help shed light on how the area's cottage industries gave way to the power of expanding cities such as Sheffield(n)

programme.title: Time Team
programme.desc: Tony Robinson and the team search for Roman artefacts beneath the graveyard of St Kyneburgha's Church in Castor, Cambridgeshire. With several reports of similar finds in and around the village, speculation mounts that they could uncover the remnants of a praetorium - a huge building that would have dominated the region's skyline during the period. However, as the experts start digging in other nearby locations, they discover that not all the evidence points to the same conclusion(n)

programme.desc: Hairdresser Julie hosts the first of a week of dinner parties in Co Durham, hoping to win over her guests with a no-nonsense menu she can prepare in advance. programme.title: Jade: The Reality Star Who Changed Britain
programme.desc: Ten years on from her death, this series explores the rollercoaster life of Jade Goody, one of the most loved - and hated - reality TV stars, to tell a bigger story of class, politics and cultural change in Britain, at a time when reality TV became the biggest and most polarising form of entertainment in the country. programme.title: Horizon
programme.sub-title: Horizon: OCD - A Monster in My Mind
programme.desc: Developmental psychologist Uta Frith, a professor of neuroscience at University College London, sets out to dispel misconceptions about Obsessive Compulsive Disorder, which some regard as simply being overly fussy about tidiness, but which is often a much more serious, and potentially crippling brain disorder. She introduces Sophie, who must constantly check she hasn't killed people wherever she goes, and Richard, who is terrified of coming into contact with bins. Also featured is the story of Nanda, who is undergoing pioneering brain surgery to stop her worrying about 'components' on her body. Having explored the impact OCD can have on people's lives, Uta investigates therapy available to those struggling with the condition, and outlines whether neuroscience can offer anything by way of a cure(n)

programme.title: Dukes, Hustlers and the Real Casino Royale
programme.desc: On his death in 2000, John Aspinall was best known for his love of wildlife, but in the 1960s he ran world-famous casino the Clermont Club in London's Mayfair. There he created a gambling world of glamour and excitement, with aristocrats, film stars, MPs and millionaire businessmen arriving nightly. However, it has been claimed that Aspinall's involvement with gangland boss Billy Hill led to cheating at the card tables - although Aspinall's family and friends deny any criminal activity. This film mixes testimony of club members with dramatic reconstructions to tell the story of the alleged con(n)

programme.desc: The remains of London's oldest prison on Millbank prompt Steve Brooker to try out solitary confinement, while Johnny Vaughan investigates forms of torture(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Documentary investigating scientific breakthroughs in research on Alzheimer's disease, which are bringing hope to millions of sufferers across the world. New scanning and gene technology is allowing scientists to identify the disease at its earliest stages, often 15 years before symptoms appear and brain cells are destroyed, with drug trials in Colombia, USA and Europe showing startling success. The programme also reveals the changes in lifestyle that can prevent the development of the disease, as well as the effects of a UK-wide trial(n)

programme.title: London Live News
programme.desc: News headlines, travel, weather and debate from across the capital(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: A property expert offers advice to British householders aiming to buy a second home abroad(n)

programme.title: The Rise of Female Violence
programme.desc: Alys Harte investigates the rising number of females who are the instigators and perpetrators of violent acts, from women who beat their boyfriends, to drunken brawlers and teenage girl gangs, and also hears from the victims of such assaults(n)

programme.title: Make Bradford British
programme.desc: Part one of two. Observational documentary exploring the issue of what it means to be British for Bradford residents who, despite sharing the same nationality, are all from different races and backgrounds. The experiment begins with a cross-section of citizens from the city taking a government test to demonstrate their knowledge of British culture. Eight people who fail the test, including pub landlady Audrey, taxi driver Mohammed and former magistrate Maura, are then placed in pairs and encouraged to swap lifestyles under the supervision of two experts in community relations(n)

programme.title: Long Weekend
programme.desc: A couple take a camping holiday at a remote beach to try and rebuild their struggling relationship. Their disrespect for the wildlife and the environment provokes the wrath of a supernatural presence, and the torment they endure from it brings tensions between them to the surface. Horror, starring John Hargreaves and Briony Behets(n)

programme.title: Total Wipeout
programme.desc: Adventure game show in which 20 men and women travel to Argentina to compete in physically demanding challenges on a purpose-built obstacle course. Richard Hammond presents from the safety of the studio, while Amanda Byram meets the contestants on the course itself(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz show in which pairs of contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions posed to 100 people before the show. At the end of each round, the team with the most points is eliminated until the final two pairs battle for the chance to compete for a cash prize. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)
